title: adenocalymma aspericarpum
intro: Adenocalymma aspericarpum, also known as the 'Wild Jasmine', is a plant species belonging to the family Bignoniaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America and has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its flavonoid and phenolic compounds. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues and gastrointestinal problems. Adenocalymma aspericarpum is traditionally used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
